content:
Character: Ronan Hale
Perception: Ronan looks like he hasn't seen direct sunlight in weeks — pale, angular, always in black, with eyes that focus about six inches behind whatever he's looking at. We met at a dev meetup and discovered we're neighbors, which became an easy excuse to grab coffee. He's one of the most technically talented programmers I've met, and his game design instincts are genuinely brilliant — he once explained his boss AI system and I felt like I was listening to someone describe architecture. He's stubborn to the point of self-destruction. I think he sees me as a kindred spirit, maybe the only person in the building who understands what shipping a product costs. I know he's almost broke. I wish he'd let someone help.
summary:
Character: Ronan Hale
Perception: Ronan looks like he hasn't seen direct sunlight in weeks — pale, angular, always in black, with eyes that focus about six inches behind whatever he's looking at. We met at a dev meetup and discovered we're neighbors, which became an easy excuse to grab coffee. He's one of the most technically talented programmers I've met, and his game design instincts are genuinely brilliant — he once explained his boss AI system and I felt like I was listening to someone describe architecture. He's stubborn to the point of self-destruction. I think he sees me as a kindred spirit, maybe the only person in the building who understands what shipping a product costs. I know he's almost broke. I wish he'd let someone help.
